HTML Boilerplat.content1: HTML headings are defined with the to tags.
HTML Boilerplat.content2: defines the most important heading, while defines the least important heading.
HTML Boilerplat.question: Which heading tag represents the largest default font size?

HTML Boilerplat.content1: HTML links are defined with the tag, which stands for anchor.
HTML Boilerplat.content2: The link's destination is specified in the href attribute.
HTML Boilerplat.question: Which attribute is used to provide the URL for a link?

HTML Boilerplat.content1: HTML images are defined with the tag.
HTML Boilerplat.content2: The source file (src) and alternative text (alt) are provided as attributes.
HTML Boilerplat.question: Which tag is used to embed an image in a webpage?

HTML Boilerplat.content1: An unordered list starts with , while an ordered list starts with .
HTML Boilerplat.content2: Each list item within these tags is defined using the tag.
HTML Boilerplat.question: Which tag is used to create a bulleted (unordered) list?

HTML Boilerplat.content1: HTML tables are defined with the tag.
HTML Boilerplat.content2: Table rows are defined with and table data/cells with .
HTML Boilerplat.question: Which tag is used to define a single row in a table?

HTML Boilerplat.content1: HTML buttons are defined with the tag.
HTML Boilerplat.content2: You can put text or even images inside the button tags.
HTML Boilerplat.question: What is the correct tag for creating a clickable button?

HTML Boilerplat.content1: An HTML form is used to collect user input using the element.
HTML Boilerplat.content2: Forms contain input elements like text fields, checkboxes, and radio buttons.
HTML Boilerplat.question: Which element is the container for user input fields?

HTML Boilerplat.content1: HTML comments are not displayed in the browser.
HTML Boilerplat.content2: They are written using the syntax.
HTML Boilerplat.question: Which of these is a correct HTML comment?

HTML Boilerplat.content1: The tag inserts a single line break.
HTML Boilerplat.content2: It is an empty tag, which means that it has no closing tag.
HTML Boilerplat.question: Which tag is used to force a line break without starting a new paragraph?

HTML Boilerplat.content1: The tag is used as a container for other HTML elements.
HTML Boilerplat.content2: It is a block-level element often used for styling with CSS.
HTML Boilerplat.question: Which tag is commonly used to group large sections of HTML together?

HTML Boilerplate.content1: The <!DOCTYPE html> declaration must be the very first thing in your HTML document.
HTML Boilerplate.content2: It is not an HTML tag; it is an instruction to the browser about the document type.
HTML Boilerplate.question: Where should the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration be placed?
HTML Boilerplate.choice2: At the very top of the file

HTML Boilerplate.content1: The <html> tag wraps all other content on the page (except the doctype).
HTML Boilerplate.content2: It is known as the root element of an HTML page.
HTML Boilerplate.question: Which element is considered the 'root' of an HTML document?

HTML Boilerplate.content1: The 'lang' attribute is usually added to the <html> tag to declare the language.
HTML Boilerplate.content2: This helps search engines and screen readers identify the page language.
HTML Boilerplate.question: What is the correct way to declare English as the language?

HTML Boilerplate.content2: Content inside the head is not visible to the user on the webpage.
HTML Boilerplate.question: Is the content inside the <head> tag visible on the main page area?

HTML Boilerplate.content1: The <meta charset='UTF-8'> tag tells the browser which character set to use.
HTML Boilerplate.content2: UTF-8 covers almost all characters and symbols in the world.
HTML Boilerplate.question: Which meta tag is used to handle special characters and symbols?

HTML Boilerplate.content1: The viewport meta tag ensures your website looks good on all devices (mobile/desktop).
HTML Boilerplate.content2: It controls the dimensions and scaling of the browser window.
HTML Boilerplate.question: What is the primary purpose of the viewport meta tag?

HTML Boilerplate.content1: The <title> tag is required in all HTML documents.
HTML Boilerplate.content2: It defines the name of the page shown in the browser tab and search results.
HTML Boilerplate.question: Where does the text inside the <title> tag appear?

HTML Boilerplate.content1: The <body> element contains all the visible content of the page.
HTML Boilerplate.content2: This includes headings, paragraphs, images, and links.
HTML Boilerplate.question: Which tag contains the content that users actually see and interact with?

HTML Boilerplate.content1: The <link> tag is used to connect an external CSS file to your HTML.
HTML Boilerplate.content2: It is placed inside the <head> section and uses 'rel' and 'href' attributes.
HTML Boilerplate.question: Which attribute in the <link> tag points to the location of the CSS file?

HTML Boilerplate.content1: Some boilerplate tags, like <meta> and <link>, do not need a closing tag.
HTML Boilerplate.content2: These are called empty or self-closing elements.
HTML Boilerplate.question: Which of these boilerplate tags is a self-closing tag?

Head Metadata.content1: The <meta> tag provides structured metadata about the document.
Head Metadata.content2: It is used by browsers for rendering and by search engines for indexing.
Head Metadata.question: Where are meta tags located in the boilerplate?

Head Metadata.content1: The 'description' meta tag provides a summary of the webpage.
Head Metadata.content2: Search engines use this text to show a snippet in search results.
Head Metadata.question: Which meta 'name' is used to provide a summary for search engines?

Head Metadata.content1: The 'keywords' meta tag lists relevant words found on the page.
Head Metadata.content2: While less important today, it was once the main way to rank in search.
Head Metadata.question: How are multiple keywords separated inside the content attribute?

Head Metadata.content1: The 'author' meta tag specifies the name of the creator of the page.
Head Metadata.content2: This is useful for internal documentation and ownership tracking.
Head Metadata.question: Which meta name attribute identifies the person who wrote the code?

Head Metadata.content1: The 'http-equiv' attribute can be used to refresh the page automatically.
Head Metadata.content2: It can also redirect the user to another URL after a specific time.
Head Metadata.question: Which attribute is used to simulate an HTTP header command?

Text Basics.content1: HTML headings are defined with the <h1> to <h6> tags.
Text Basics.content2: <h1> defines the most important heading, while <h6> defines the least important.
Text Basics.question: Which heading tag has the largest default font size?

Text Basics.content1:
The

tag is used to define a paragraph of text.
Text Basics.content2: Browsers automatically add some white space (margin) before and after each paragraph.
Text Basics.question: Which tag is the standard way to wrap a block of text?

Text Basics.content1: The <hr> tag defines a thematic break in an HTML page.
Text Basics.content2: It is most often displayed as a horizontal line that separates content.
Text Basics.question: Which tag is used to draw a straight line across the page?

Text Basics.content1:
The
tag inserts a single line break without starting a new paragraph.
Text Basics.content2: It is an empty tag, which means it does not have a closing tag.
Text Basics.question: Which tag would you use to move text to the next line immediately?

Text Basics.content2: Text inside <pre> is displayed in a fixed-width font and preserves spaces and line breaks.
Text Basics.question: Which tag keeps all your spaces and tabs exactly as you typed them?

Formatting.content1: The <b> tag makes text bold without any extra importance.
Formatting.content2: The <strong> tag also makes text bold but indicates that the text is of great importance.
Formatting.question: Which tag should you use for text that is 'strong' in importance?

Formatting.content1: The <i> tag makes text italic without extra meaning.
Formatting.content2: The <em> tag defines emphasized text, which is typically displayed in italics.
Formatting.question: Which tag is used to 'emphasize' text for screen readers and browsers?

Formatting.content1: The <small> tag defines smaller text, often used for side comments.
Formatting.content2: The <mark> tag defines text that should be highlighted with a background color.
Formatting.question: Which tag is used to 'highlight' or mark parts of a text?

Formatting.content1: The <del> tag defines text that has been deleted from a document.
Formatting.content2: The <ins> tag defines text that has been inserted, usually shown with an underline.
Formatting.question: Which tag adds a strikethrough line over the text?

Formatting.content1: The <sub> tag defines subscript text (appears half a character below the line).
Formatting.content2: The <sup> tag defines superscript text (appears half a character above the line).
Formatting.question: Which tag is used for exponents or powers in math (like 10²)?

lists.content1: An unordered list starts with the <ul> tag.
lists.content2: The list items are marked with bullets (small black circles) by default.
lists.question: Which tag is used to create a bulleted (unordered) list?

lists.content1: An ordered list starts with the <ol> tag.
lists.content2: The list items are marked with numbers (1, 2, 3...) by default.
lists.question: Which tag is used to create a numbered list?

lists.content1: Each item within a list is defined using the <li> tag.
lists.content2: The <li> tag must be placed inside either a <ul> or an <ol> container.
lists.question: What does the 'li' in the <li> tag stand for?

lists.content1: A description list is a list of terms, with a description of each term.
lists.content2: It uses the <dl> tag for the list, <dt> for the term, and <dd> for the description.
lists.question: Which tag is used to define the actual 'description' in a description list?

lists.content1: Lists can be nested, meaning one list can be placed inside another list item.
lists.content2: This is commonly used to create multi-level navigation menus or outlines.
lists.question: Where should a new <ul> start if you want to nest it inside another list?

links.content1: HTML links are defined with the <a> tag, which stands for 'anchor'.
links.content2: The text between the opening and closing tags is what the user clicks on.
links.question: Which tag is used to create a hyperlink?

links.content1: The 'href' attribute is the most important part of the link.
links.content2: It indicates the destination address or URL where the link goes.
links.question: Which attribute specifies the web address for a link?

links.content1: The 'target' attribute specifies where to open the linked document.
links.content2: Using target='_blank' will open the linked document in a new window or tab.
links.question: Which target value opens a link in a new browser tab?

links.content1: You can create a link that opens the user's email program to send a message.
links.content2: This is done by using 'mailto:' followed by the email address inside the href.
links.question: What prefix is needed in the href to create an email link?

links.content1: You can turn an image into a link by nesting the tag inside an tag.
links.content2: When the user clicks the image, they will be taken to the destination in the href.
links.question: How do you make an image clickable as a link?

images.content1: HTML images are defined with the tag.
images.content2: The tag is empty, which means it contains attributes only and has no closing tag.
images.question: Which of these is a correct characteristic of the tag?

images.content1: The 'src' attribute specifies the path (URL) to the image file.
images.content2: If the browser cannot find the image, it will display a broken link icon.
images.question: Which attribute points to the location of the image file?

images.content1: The 'alt' attribute provides an alternate text for an image.
images.content2: It is used by screen readers and displayed if the image fails to load.
images.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'alt' attribute?

images.content1: You can use the 'width' and 'height' attributes to specify the size of an image.
images.content2: The values are specified in pixels by default without needing to add 'px'.
images.question: In which unit are the width and height attributes measured?

images.content1: HTML supports many image formats including JPG, PNG, GIF, and SVG.
images.content2: Choosing the right format helps with page loading speed and image quality.
images.question: Which of these is a common web image format?

tables.content1: An HTML table is defined with the <table> tag.
tables.content2: It acts as a container for rows, headers, and data cells.
tables.question: Which tag is the main wrapper for an HTML table?

tables.content1: Each table row is defined with the <tr> tag.
tables.content2: A table can have any number of rows depending on the data.
tables.question: What does the 'tr' in the <tr> tag stand for?

tables.content1: Table headers are defined with the <th> tag.
tables.content2: By default, browsers display table headers as bold and centered.
tables.question: Which tag is used to define a header cell in a table?

tables.content1: Table cells (data) are defined with the <td> tag.
tables.content2: These tags are nested inside the <tr> tags to hold the actual content.
tables.question: Which tag is used for standard data cells in a table?

tables.content1: The colspan attribute allows a cell to span across multiple columns.
tables.content2: The rowspan attribute allows a cell to span across multiple rows.
tables.question: Which attribute would you use to make one cell take up two columns?

forms.content1: The HTML <form> element is used to create a form for user input.
forms.content2: It acts as a container for different types of input elements like text fields and checkboxes.
forms.question: Which tag is used to wrap all the input elements of a form?

forms.content1: The 'action' attribute defines the action to be performed when the form is submitted.
forms.content2: Usually, the form data is sent to a file on the server when the user clicks submit.
forms.question: Which attribute tells the form where to send the data?

forms.content1: The 'method' attribute specifies the HTTP method to be used when submitting the form data.
forms.content2: The most common methods used are GET and POST.
forms.question: Which attribute specifies how the form data should be sent?

forms.content1: The 'target' attribute specifies if the submitted result will open in a new browser tab.
forms.content2: The default value is '_self', which means the form will be submitted in the current window.
forms.question: Which target value opens the form response in a new tab?

forms.content1: The <fieldset> tag is used to group related data in a form.
forms.content2: The <legend> tag defines a caption for the <fieldset> element.
forms.question: Which tag provides a title or caption for a grouped set of form fields?

input types.content1: The <input type='text'> defines a single-line input field for text input.
input types.content2: It is the most common input type and is used for names, usernames, and search terms.
input types.question: Which input type is used for a standard one-line text box?

input types.content1: The <input type='password'> defines a password field.
input types.content2: The characters in a password field are masked (shown as asterisks or circles).
input types.question: Which input type hides the characters as they are typed?

input types.content1: The <input type='checkbox'> lets a user select ZERO or MORE options of a limited number of choices.
input types.content2: Checkboxes are independent of each other, unlike radio buttons.
input types.question: Which input type allows selecting multiple options at once?

input types.content1: The <input type='radio'> lets a user select ONLY ONE of a limited number of choices.
input types.content2: To group radio buttons, they must all share the same 'name' attribute.
input types.question: Which input type restricts the user to picking only one option?

input types.content1: The <input type='submit'> defines a button for submitting form data to a form-handler.
input types.content2: The form-handler is typically a file on the server specified in the form's action attribute.
input types.question: Which input type sends the collected form data to the server?

labels & selects.content1: The <label> tag defines a caption for many form elements.
labels & selects.content2: Clicking the text inside the <label> element toggles the control it is linked to.
labels & selects.question: Which attribute is used to link a label to an input's ID?

labels & selects.content1: The <select> element is used to create a drop-down list.
labels & selects.content2: It is most often used in a form to collect user input from a list of options.
labels & selects.question: Which tag is the main container for a dropdown menu?

labels & selects.content1: The <option> tag defines an item in the drop-down list.
labels & selects.content2: By default, the first item in the <select> list is selected.
labels & selects.question: Which tag defines the individual choices inside a select menu?

labels & selects.content1: Use the 'size' attribute to specify the number of visible options.
labels & selects.content2: If the size is greater than 1, the select box will look like a scrollable list.
labels & selects.question: Which attribute changes how many options are visible at once?

labels & selects.content1: The 'multiple' attribute allows the user to select more than one value.
labels & selects.content2: Users typically hold down Ctrl or Command to select multiple items.
labels & selects.question: Which attribute allows selecting several options from a dropdown?

buttons.content1: The <button> tag defines a clickable button.
buttons.content2: Unlike the input-based button, you can put content like text or images inside it.
buttons.question: What is the primary tag used to create a clickable button?

buttons.content1: The 'type' attribute specifies the behavior of the button.
buttons.content2: Common types include 'submit' for forms and 'button' for general actions.
buttons.question: Which button type is the default behavior inside a form?

buttons.content1: The 'disabled' attribute is a boolean attribute.
buttons.content2: When present, it specifies that the button should be unusable and unclickable.
buttons.question: Which attribute prevents a user from clicking a button?

buttons.content1: Buttons have default styles provided by the browser.
buttons.content2: These styles can be completely overridden using CSS for a custom look.
buttons.question: Can the default appearance of an HTML button be changed?

buttons.content1: The <button type='reset'> is used to clear all inputs in a form.
buttons.content2: It restores all form fields to their initial default values.
buttons.question: Which button type clears all data entered into a form?

containers (div & span).content1: The <div> element is a block-level container.
containers (div & span).content2: It is used to group large sections of HTML elements for styling with CSS.
containers (div & span).question: Which element is a block-level container for other HTML tags?

containers (div & span).content1: The <span> element is an inline container used to mark up a part of a text.
containers (div & span).content2: It does not start on a new line and only takes up as much width as necessary.
containers (div & span).question: Which tag is best for styling a specific word inside a paragraph?

containers (div & span).content1: Block elements (div) always start on a new line and take up the full width.
containers (div & span).content2: Inline elements (span) stay on the same line and only take the width of their content.
containers (div & span).question: Which of these is a key difference between <div> and <span>?
containers (div & span).choice1: Div is block; Span is inline
containers (div & span).choice2: Div is inline; Span is block

containers (div & span).content1: You can nest multiple <div> elements inside each other to create complex layouts.
containers (div & span).content2: This is a fundamental practice in creating modern web structures.
containers (div & span).question: Is it valid to put one <div> inside another <div>?

containers (div & span).content1: Containers like <div> and <span> often use the 'class' or 'id' attributes.
containers (div & span).content2: These attributes allow developers to target the containers for CSS and JS.
containers (div & span).question: Which attribute is most commonly used to group multiple <div> elements for CSS?

semantic html.content2: It usually contains a group of introductory or navigational aids.
semantic html.question: Which tag is used for the introductory section of a page or section?

semantic html.content1: The <nav> element defines a set of navigation links.
semantic html.content2: It is intended only for major blocks of navigation links.
semantic html.question: Which semantic tag is best for a menu of website links?

semantic html.content1: The <main> tag specifies the main content of a document.
semantic html.content2: The content inside should be unique to the document.
semantic html.question: How many <main> elements should be visible on a single page?

attributes (id & class).content1: The 'id' attribute specifies a unique id for an HTML element.
attributes (id & class).content2: The value must be unique within the HTML document.
attributes (id & class).question: Can two different elements on the same page have the same 'id'?

attributes (id & class).content1: The 'class' attribute is used to define equal styles for elements.
attributes (id & class).content2: Multiple HTML elements can share the same class.
attributes (id & class).question: Which attribute is used to group many elements for the same CSS style?

multimedia.content1: The <video> element is used to show a video on a webpage.
multimedia.content2: It uses the <source> tag to point to the video file.
multimedia.question: Which element is used to embed video content?

multimedia.content1: The <audio> element is used to play sound files.
multimedia.content2: It supports formats like MP3, WAV, and OGG.
multimedia.question: Which tag is used to embed sound on a page?

entities.content1: Some characters are reserved in HTML, like the less than () sign?

seo & accessibility.content1: ARIA roles provide further meaning to elements for screen readers.
seo & accessibility.content2: They help users with disabilities navigate complex interfaces.
seo & accessibility.question: What does ARIA stand for?
